description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust wooden handle with a natural finish and a slight ergonomic curve for improved grip. The head of the axe is made of metal, painted in a bright orange color with a silver cutting edge, making it highly visible and suitable for safety in urban environments. The blade is broad and flat on one side, with a pronounced poll on the opposite end, ideal for splitting or chopping tasks. The handle appears to be securely fitted into the axe head, likely using a traditional wedged mounting style. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This tool is commonly found in garages, maintenance sheds, or used by municipal workers for various cutting and splitting tasks.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a bright orange metal head with a silver edge, designed for chopping and splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the orange axe head and the straight-on profile of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the handle and the thickness of the axe head, with the orange and silver blade profile visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the width of the blade and the handle's attachment into the head.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the handle and the back of the axe head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad blade with a flat edge, silver cutting surface, orange painted body, and a pronounced poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the axe head.
